name: Freezing Combination Main Dishes (Usda)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare your main dish as you normally would, cooking it until almost done.
2. Allow the food to cool completely before proceeding with freezing.
3. Divide the prepared food into four parts of six servings each using an 8x8-inch pan or similar size for easier handling.
4. Wrap each part tightly in freezer paper, polyethylene film, or heavy-weight aluminum foil to prevent exposure to air and moisture loss during storage.
5. Label each package with the name of the dish, date of freezing, and the last recommended use date for best quality.
6. Place the wrapped packages into the freezer at a temperature of 0°F or below.
Storage:
Store the frozen main dishes in the freezer at 0°F or below. Most combination main dishes can be stored for up to 6 months without significant loss of quality, unless otherwise specified.
Preparation for Serving:
1. Preheat your oven according to the recipe instructions.
2. Remove any wrappings from the desired portion and place it into an 8x8-inch baking pan or the same size ovenproof pan used during freezing.
3. Place the pan in the preheated oven and heat until thoroughly cooked.
4. Thaw salads in the refrigerator before serving; avoid thawing at room temperature to maintain food quality.
5. Leftovers can be stored in the refrigerator for a day or two, but should not be refrozen after thawing.
